quality.rationale: 结合ADM结条件与四维速度归一化推导薄壳运动方程，涉及真实引力坍缩物理模型与常微分方程积分

origin.solution: Solution 21.11. If $\tau$ is proper time measured by an observer at rest in the dust, then the motion of the shell is specified by $\mathbf{r}(\tau)$ , where $\mathbf{r}$ is the radial coordinate of the shell in either the interior or exterior metric. The metric of the shell can be specified by$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {d} \tau^{2} + \mathrm {R}^{2} (\tau) \left(\mathrm {d} \theta^{2} + \sin^{2} \theta \mathrm {d} \phi^{2}\right).$$Here $4\pi \mathbf{R}^2 =$ the surface area of the shell at $\pmb{\tau}$ , and thus $\mathbb{R}(\pmb {\tau}) = \mathbb{r}(\pmb {\tau})$From the second relation in Problem 21.10 we have that$$0 = \frac {\mathrm {d} \sigma}{\mathrm {d} \tau} + \sigma \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {i}}_{\mathrm {i}} = (\sigma \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {i}})_{\mathrm {i}} = (\sigma (^{(3)} \mathrm {g})^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {i}})_{, i} / (^{(3)} \mathrm {g})^{\frac {1}{2}}.$$But $\mathbf{u}^{\tau} = 1$ and $^{(3)}\mathbf{g} = \mathbb{R}^{4}(\tau)$ so that $(\sigma \mathbb{R}^2)_\tau = 0$ . This implies that $4\pi \mathbb{R}^2\sigma = \mu$ , which we identify with the rest mass of the shell, is a constant.To find the equation of motion we use the junction condition from Problem 21.10$$\left[ \mathrm {K}_{\theta \theta}\right] = 8 \pi \sigma \left(\mathrm {u}_{\theta} \mathrm {u}_{\theta} + \frac {1}{2}^{(3)} \mathrm {g}_{\theta \theta}\right) = 4 \pi \sigma^{(3)} \mathrm {g}_{\theta \theta} = \mu .$$Evaluating $\mathbf{K}_{\theta \theta}$ we have$$\mathrm {K}_{\theta \theta} = - \mathrm {n}_{\theta ; \theta} = \mathrm {n}_{\alpha} \Gamma_{\theta \theta}^{\alpha} = - \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {n}^{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {g}_{\theta \theta , \mathrm {r}} = - \mathrm {r} \mathrm {n}^{\mathrm {r}}$$and$$\left[ \mathrm {K}_{\theta \theta}\right] = - \mathrm {r} \left(\mathrm {n}^{\mathrm {r} +} - \mathrm {n}^{\mathrm {r} -}\right) = \mu$$where $\mathfrak{n}^{r+}$ and $\mathfrak{n}^{r-}$ are the radial components of the normal evaluated in the exterior and the interior geometry.By using $\mathbf{u} \cdot \mathbf{n} = 0$ and $\mathbf{n} \cdot \mathbf{n} = -\mathbf{u} \cdot \mathbf{u} = 1$ , we can evaluate the components of $\mathbf{u}$ and $\mathbf{n}$ . Exterior to the shell we have$$\begin{array}{l}1 = \left(1 - \frac {2 M}{r}\right) \left(u^{t}\right)^{2} - \left(u^{r}\right)^{2} / \left(1 - \frac {2 M}{r}\right) \\0 = n_{r} u^{r} + n_{t} u^{t} \\1 = - \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{- 1} \left(\mathrm {n}_{\mathrm {t}}\right)^{2} + \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}}\right) \left(\mathrm {n}_{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{2}, \\\end{array}$$and by eliminating $\mathbf{u}^{\mathrm{t}}, \mathbf{n}_{\mathrm{t}}$ we find$$n_{r}^{+} = \left[ \frac {1 + (u^{r})^{2} / \left(1 - \frac {2 M}{r}\right)}{\left(1 - \frac {2 M}{r}\right)}\right]^{\frac {1}{2}}.$$On the shell $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{R}(r)$ and $\mathbf{u}^{\mathrm{r}} = \mathrm{d}\mathbf {R} / \mathrm {d}r\equiv \dot{\mathbf {R}}$ so the contravariant component of $\mathbf{n}$ , exterior to the shell is$$\mathbf {n}^{\mathbf {r} +} = \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathbf {M}}{\mathbf {R}} + \dot {\mathbf {R}}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}.$$The calculation for $\mathfrak{n}^{\mathbf{r} - }$ is identical, and we get the same answer as above with M set to zero. Thus we have$$\mu = - \mathsf {R} (\mathsf {n}^{\mathsf {r} +} - \mathsf {n}^{\mathsf {r} -}) = - \mathsf {R} \bigg \{\left[ 1 - \frac {2 \mathsf {M}}{\mathsf {R}} + \dot {\mathsf {R}}^{2}\right]^{\frac {1}{2}} - \left[ 1 + \dot {\mathsf {R}}^{2}\right]^{\frac {1}{2}} \bigg \}$$which can be solved for $\mathbf{M}$ to give the equation of motion$$\mathrm {M} = \mu \left(1 + \dot {\mathrm {R}}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - \mu^{2} / 2 \mathrm {R}.$$If $\mathbf {R} = 0$ at $\mathbf {R} = \inf ty$ (i.e. infall from rest at $\inf ty$ ), then $\mathbf {M} = \mu$ and$$\dot {\mathbf {R}} = - \frac {\mu}{2 \mathbf {R}} \left(1 + \frac {4 \mathbf {R}}{\mu}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}.$$This can easily be integrated to give$$\frac {4}{\mu} \tau = - \frac {1}{3} \left(1 + \frac {4 \mathrm {R}}{\mu}\right)^{\frac {3}{2}} + \left(1 + \frac {4 \mathrm {R}}{\mu}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - \frac {2}{3},$$where we have taken $\tau = 0$ to correspond to $\mathbf {R} = 0$ .

killing_vectors.name: time_translation
